title: Cache Performance, Scope, Integrity, and Poisoning Defense · Delivery Incident Triage
summary: A release responder inherits a plausible but defective cache threat and measurement policy; preserve run evidence, isolate the first causal fault in cache performance, scope, integrity, and poisoning defense, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in cache threat and measurement policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Separate disposable performance caches from immutable artifacts, job outputs, external registries, and release records by trust and lifetime.
Construct keys from OS, architecture, tool, dependency lock, relevant configuration, and cache version with bounded restore fallback.
Restrict who can save or restore executable caches across fork, branch, event, workflow, and privilege boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence
steps.instruction:
Order the Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence delivery-system evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence, keep Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: A debug privileged release restores executable cache content written by a fork through a broad fallback key. Compare cold and warm time, key scope, save authority, poisoning risk, and immutable artifact separation. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug privileged release restores executable cache content written by a fork through a broad fallback key. Compare cold and warm time, key scope, save authority, poisoning risk, and immutable artifact separation. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run.

For Order the regression proof · Debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A warm-cache improvement proves the complete pipeline is faster.

options.text: Capture event, workflow and revision identity, actor, permissions, runner, toolchain, dependency, artifact, environment, and release state for the debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence case
Predict expression, shell, graph, trust, cache, artifact, deployment, user-signal, failure, and cleanup effects before mutation for the debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence case
Review the smallest workflow, policy, build, attestation, or release increment in the deterministic browser simulator for the debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence case
Inject one fault, record causal evidence, repair and rollback, then name authorized disposable delivery-environment transfer evidence for the debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence case

The Ci Cache Artifact Distinction record says cache threat and measurement policy looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Ci Cache Artifact Distinction.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-cold-warm-performance-evidence-lx8r2z". Tailor its cache-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence, keep Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-cold-warm-performance-evidence-lx8r2z
  - competency: ci-cold-warm-performance-evidence
    artifact: cache-policy.yaml
    decision: cold warm performance evidence
    environment: protected main branch under a merge queue
    fault-injection: remove one supported Node and operating-system matrix cell
    retained-proof: runner image runtime version authority network and teardown record
    case-key: lx8r2z
    change: |
      cache-content: npm-downloads-only
      key: os-arch-node24-lockhash-config
      restore-prefix: exact-trusted-branch
      writer: trusted-push-only
      untrusted-reader: bounded-read
      node-modules: excluded
      privileged-release-cache: disabled
      cold-run: scheduled
      measurement: queue-install-build-test-upload
    verify: compare cold warm and poisoned-key fixtures while checking writer event scope restored key and critical path
    failure: Reject cache as provenance, executable fork-writable caches, broad restore keys, node_modules reuse, or warm-only evidence.
    repair: Cache disposable inputs, bind keys and writers, isolate trust domains, retain cold runs, and measure the actual critical path.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence structural check is only one gate. cache threat and measurement policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run.
